---
title: "CISP 360 \n Structured Programming"
subtitle: "Introduction to C++"
author: "Dr. Caleb Fowler"
version: "1.0.1"
# Changelog:
# 1.0.0 (2026-02-03) - Added versioning to YAML to keep track across lectures.
# 1.0.1 (2026-02-03) - Disable 'O' for overview 

format:
  revealjs:
    margin: 0.05      # A touch more space on slide
    css: ../styles/styles.css
    theme: serif
    slide-number: true
    navigation-mode: vertical
    transition: slide
    incremental: false
    scrollable: false
    controls: true
    hash: true
    history: true
    title-slide: false
    footer: "CISP 360 · Fowler"
    keyboard: true  # Keep this as true to enable the system
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script>
          window.Reveal.on('ready', event => {
            Reveal.configure({
              keyboard: {
                34: 'down',   // Forward button
                33: 'up',     // Back button
                116: 'right',  // Start/Play button
                190: 'left'    // Black Screen button
                79: null        // Disable 'O' for overview
              }
            });
          });
          </script>

engine: jupyter
execute:
  eval: true
  echo: true

resources:
 - topics/**/images/**

---

## Last Time

::: {.incremental}

- The while loop — entry-condition, priming read
- The do-while loop — exit-condition, menus
- The for loop — counter-controlled
- Which loop to use?

:::


## Today's Agenda

- Validating user input
- Range checking — Gauntlet of Trust
- What's the Output? (Exercise 1)
- Input validation with the while loop
- Increment & decrement operators
- Breaking out of a loop
- What's the Output? (Exercise 2)

::: notes

Timing estimate: ~45 slides at 1 min/slide = 45 min. Both desk-check exercises will run longer in practice (~10 min each). Plan for a full class.

:::


{{< include topics/topic_1_validating_input.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_2_range_checking.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_3_whats_the_output_1.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_4_input_validation_while.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_5_increment_decrement.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_6_breaking_out.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_7_whats_the_output_2.qmd >}}


# Key Takeaways

Input validation protects the program from bad data — always validate before processing. Use `++` and `--` for clean loop counters. Use `break` to exit a loop when the condition is best expressed inside the body.


# Next Time

Counters, running totals, and sentinel values.
